title: Variables
description: What variables are, how they store values, and how to use them to build a chatbot in Scratch.

steps.title: Do Now Activity
steps.content:
Do Now: variables in a story

A variable is a named container that stores a value. Assign a value to each variable below — then watch the story update automatically.

COUNTRYVILLAINTOWNSISTERHERO
Fill in all five variables above to see the story.
steps.js: (function(){var ids=['country','villain','town','sister','hero'];function v(id){return document.getElementById('dn-'+id).value.trim()||'['+id.toUpperCase()+']';}function update(){var c=v('country'),vi=v('villain'),t=v('town'),s=v('sister'),h=v('hero');document.getElementById('dn-story').innerHTML='In a faraway land called '+c+', there was a magical castle in the town of '+t+'. It was said that behind its walls lived a princess called '+h+'. '+h+' tried to hide her powers from the people of '+t+', but a prince called '+vi+' found out and forced '+h+' to run away. '+h+' imposed an endless winter on '+t+'. Her sister '+s+' set off on a dangerous adventure to bring '+h+' home to '+t+'.';var filled=ids.every(function(id){return document.getElementById('dn-'+id).value.trim()!=='';});document.getElementById('dn-done').disabled=!filled;}ids.forEach(function(id){document.getElementById('dn-'+id).addEventListener('input',update);});document.getElementById('dn-done').onclick=function(){__markStepComplete();};update();})();

steps.title: What is a Variable?
steps.content:
What is a variable?

A variable is a named storage location inside the computer's memory. It holds a value that the program can read or change at any time.

Think of it like a labelled box — the label is the variable name, and what is inside is the value:

Variable nameValue stored
name"Alex"
score250
age13

Key facts

  • A variable has a name chosen by the programmer
  • A variable stores a value — a number or a piece of text
  • The value can change while the program runs
  • The name always refers to the same storage location

steps.title: Input → Process → Output
steps.content:
Input → Process → Output

Every computer program follows this pattern. Variables live in storage — they hold values between the input and output steps.

INPUT
data coming in
keyboard, mouse,
microphone
→
PROCESS
code runs,
calculations happen,
decisions made
→
OUTPUT
result shown
screen, speaker,
printer
STORAGE (variables)
values saved in memory and used across the program

In the chatbot you are about to build:

  • Input — the user types their name
  • Process — the program stores it in a variable
  • Output — the sprite says hello using the stored name

steps.title: Variables in Scratch
steps.content:
Key blocks for variables

Three blocks work together to ask a question, store the answer, and use it. Order matters — each block depends on the one before it.

1. Ask a question — pauses the program and waits for the user to type. The answer is automatically stored in a built-in variable called answer.

ask [What is your name?] and wait

2. Save the answer — copy the value from answer into a variable you have created and named.

set [name v] to (answer)

3. Use the variable — the join block combines fixed text with your variable into one message.

say (join [Hello ] (name)) for (2) seconds

Put them all together under when green flag clicked:

when green flag clicked
ask [What is your name?] and wait
set [name v] to (answer)
say (join [Hello ] (name)) for (2) seconds

steps.title: Predict the Output
steps.content:
Predict the output

Read this script carefully. Trace through each block in order from top to bottom.

when green flag clicked
ask [What is your name?] and wait
set [name v] to (answer)
say (join [Hello ] (name)) for (2) seconds

The user types Sam when the question appears. What does the sprite say?
steps.js: createMCQ('predict-q',['Hello Sam','Hello answer','What is your name?','Hello name'],0,'Correct! The join block combines the text "Hello " with the value stored in name — which is "Sam".','Not quite. Trace through: ask stores the typed word in answer, set copies it into name, then say uses name.');

steps.title: Build: Ask Name
steps.content:
Build it: ask_name

Build the script below in TurboWarp. Follow each step in order.

  1. Go to Variables and click Make a Variable — name it name
  2. Add a when green flag clicked block (Events)
  3. Attach ask [What is your name?] and wait (Sensing)
  4. Attach set [name] to (answer) (Variables)
  5. Attach say (join [Hello ] (name)) for (2) seconds (Looks + Operators)
  6. Click the green flag and type your name to test it

Your finished script

when green flag clicked
ask [What is your name?] and wait
set [name v] to (answer)
say (join [Hello ] (name)) for (2) seconds
steps.js: (function(){var NW=1100,NH=650;function scaleFrame(){var frame=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame');if(!frame)return;var wrap=frame.parentElement;var bar=wrap.querySelector('.y7-check-bar');var barH=bar?bar.offsetHeight:44;var isFs=wrap.classList.contains('y7-fullscreen');var availW=isFs?window.innerWidth:wrap.clientWidth;var scale=isFs?Math.min(availW/NW,(window.innerHeight-barH)/NH):availW/NW;frame.style.width=NW+'px';frame.style.height=NH+'px';frame.style.transform='scale('+scale+')';frame.style.marginBottom=Math.round(NH*(scale-1))+'px';frame.style.marginLeft=isFs?Math.max(0,Math.round((availW-NW*scale)/2))+'px':'0';}function toggleFs(){var frame=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame');var wrap=frame.parentElement;var btn=document.getElementById('y7-fs-btn');var isFs=wrap.classList.toggle('y7-fullscreen');btn.textContent=isFs?'✕':'⛶';btn.title=isFs?'Exit full screen (Esc)':'Full screen';if(isFs){window._y7ScrollY=window.scrollY;document.body.style.overflow='hidden';}else{document.body.style.overflow='';window.scrollTo(0,window._y7ScrollY||0);}scaleFrame();}scaleFrame();if(window._y7Resize)window.removeEventListener('resize',window._y7Resize);window._y7Resize=scaleFrame;window.addEventListener('resize',window._y7Resize);if(window._y7Key)document.removeEventListener('keydown',window._y7Key);window._y7Key=function(e){if(e.key==='Escape'){var f=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame');if(f&&f.parentElement.classList.contains('y7-fullscreen'))toggleFs();}};document.addEventListener('keydown',window._y7Key);document.getElementById('y7-fs-btn').onclick=toggleFs;document.getElementById('y7-check-btn').onclick=function(){var frame=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame');var result=document.getElementById('y7-check-result');try{var vm=frame.contentWindow.vm;if(!vm){result.style.color='#fca5a5';result.textContent='Editor still loading — wait a moment and try again.';return;}var targets=vm.runtime.targets;var hasAsk=false,hasSet=false,hasSay=false;for(var t=0;t<targets.length;t++){var blks=targets[t].blocks._blocks;for(var id in blks){var b=blks[id];if(b.opcode==='sensing_askandwait')hasAsk=true;if(b.opcode==='data_setvariableto')hasSet=true;if(b.opcode==='looks_sayforsecs')hasSay=true;}}if(hasAsk&&hasSet&&hasSay){result.style.color='#86efac';result.textContent='✓ Great work! Your chatbot asks, stores and says hello.';__markStepComplete();}else{var miss=[];if(!hasAsk)miss.push('ask [] and wait');if(!hasSet)miss.push('set [] to (answer)');if(!hasSay)miss.push('say [] for [] seconds');result.style.color='#fca5a5';result.textContent='Still missing: '+miss.join(', ')+'.';};}catch(e){result.style.color='#fca5a5';result.textContent='Could not check: '+e.message;console.error(e);}};})();

steps.title: Build: Ask Age
steps.content:
Build it: ask_age

Now apply the same pattern to ask for an age. This time there are two say blocks.

  1. Make a new variable called age
  2. Add when green flag clicked
  3. Attach ask [How old are you?] and wait
  4. Attach set [age] to (answer)
  5. Attach say (join [Wow ] (age)) for (2) seconds
  6. Attach say [Your monster is much older than me in the year 2182] for (2) seconds

Your finished script

when green flag clicked
ask [How old are you?] and wait
set [age v] to (answer)
say (join [Wow ] (age)) for (2) seconds
say [Your monster is much older than me in the year 2182] for (2) seconds

f=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame');if(f&&f.parentElement.classList.contains('y7-fullscreen'))toggleFs();}};document.addEventListener('keydown',window._y7Key);document.getElementById('y7-fs-btn').onclick=toggleFs;document.getElementById('y7-check-btn').onclick=function(){var vm=frame.contentWindow.vm;if(!vm){result.style.color='#fca5a5';result.textContent='Editor still loading — wait a moment and try again.';return;}var targets=vm.runtime.targets;var sayCount=0,hasAsk=false,hasSet=false;for(var t=0;t<targets.length;t++){var blks=targets[t].blocks._blocks;for(var id in blks){var b=blks[id];if(b.opcode==='sensing_askandwait')hasAsk=true;if(b.opcode==='data_setvariableto')hasSet=true;if(b.opcode==='looks_sayforsecs')sayCount++;}}if(hasAsk&&hasSet&&sayCount>=2){result.style.color='#86efac';result.textContent='✓ Perfect! Two say blocks, an ask and a set — well done.';__markStepComplete();}else{var miss=[];if(!hasAsk)miss.push('ask block');if(!hasSet)miss.push('set variable');if(sayCount<2)miss.push('two say blocks');result.style.color='#fca5a5';result.textContent='Not quite. Missing: '+miss.join(', ')+'.';};}catch(e){result.style.color='#fca5a5';result.textContent='Could not check: '+e.message;console.error(e);}};})();

steps.title: Tracing Variables
steps.content:
Tracing variables

Tracing means following a program line by line and recording what value a variable holds at each point. It is one of the most important skills in programming.

Track the value of score as each block runs. Write it down as you go.

when green flag clicked
set [score v] to (0)
say (score) for (1) seconds
set [score v] to ((score) + (10))
say (score) for (1) seconds
set [score v] to ((score) * (2))
say (score) for (1) seconds

What does the sprite say the third time it speaks?
steps.js: createMCQ('trace-q',['0','10','20','30'],2,'Correct! score starts at 0, then 0+10=10, then 10×2=20. The third say shows 20.','Not quite. Trace it step by step: set to 0 → say 0 → add 10 → say 10 → multiply by 2 → say 20.');

steps.title: End of Lesson Check
steps.content:
End of lesson check

Answer all four questions. The correct answer is shown after each one.
steps.js: createMCQSet('exam-q',[{q:'What is a variable?',opts:['A block that moves a sprite','A named storage location that holds a value','A type of loop','A sound effect'],correct:1,feedback:'Correct — a variable is a named container that stores a value in memory.',wrongFeedback:'A variable is a named storage location that holds a value. The name stays the same; the value can change.'},{q:'Which block copies the typed answer into a variable called name?',opts:['say (name) for 2 seconds','ask [name] and wait','set [name v] to (answer)','join [name] [answer]'],correct:2,feedback:'Correct — set [] to (answer) copies whatever the user typed into your named variable.',wrongFeedback:'The correct block is set [name v] to (answer). It takes the value in answer and stores it in name.'},{q:'In the IPO model, which part do variables belong to?',opts:['Input','Process','Output','Storage'],correct:3,feedback:'Correct — variables are part of storage, holding values between the input and output stages.',wrongFeedback:'Variables belong to Storage — they sit in memory and hold values that the program uses during processing.'},{q:'A script runs: set [score v] to (5), then set [score v] to ((score) + (3)). What is score?',opts:['3','5','8','15'],correct:2,feedback:'Correct! 5 + 3 = 8. The second set block adds 3 to the existing value of 5.',wrongFeedback:'Trace it: score starts at 5, then 5 + 3 = 8. The answer is 8.'}]);

options: A block that moves a sprite
A named storage location that holds a value

quizQuestions.q: Which Scratch block asks the user a question and waits for a reply?
After 'ask [] and wait' runs, where is the typed answer automatically stored?

options: Copies the value of answer into the name variable

quizQuestions.q: In the IPO model, which part do variables belong to?
What does the join block do?
options: Combines two pieces of text into one

quizQuestions.q: score is 10. After 'set [score v] to ((score) + (5))', what is score?
What is a bug in programming?

options: Finding and fixing errors in code

quizQuestions.q: Why must 'ask [] and wait' come before 'set [] to (answer)' in a script?
options: You need to ask the question before the answer exists to store
